queries.title: Circular Fund Transfers (Laundering Loop)
queries.description: Detect funds being transferred through a sequence of accounts and returning to the starting account (A -> B -> C -> A).
queries.gql:
GRAPH AMLGraph
MATCH (a:Account)-[:Transfers]->(b:Account)-[:Transfers]->(c:Account)-[:Transfers]->(a:Account)
WHERE a.account_id != b.account_id AND b.account_id != c.account_id
RETURN a.account_id AS starting_account, b.account_id AS hop_1_account, c.account_id AS hop_2_account, a.bank_name AS original_bank
queries.talk_track: This GQL query instantly exposes circular transaction loops — a classic layering technique used by money launderers to obscure fund origins. In standard relational databases, finding these loops requires highly complex, slow, and expensive recursive Common Table Expressions (CTEs). With Spanner Graph, we express this loop in a single MATCH statement, traversing multiple transactional accounts globally with sub-second latency.

queries.title: High-Risk Customer Shell Company Routing
queries.description: Identify high-risk customers (risk_score = 10) routing funds through their accounts directly into high-risk merchants (Casinos or Shell Companies).
queries.gql:
GRAPH AMLGraph
MATCH (c:Customer {risk_score: 10})-[:OwnsAccount]->(a:Account)-[:PaysMerchant]->(m:Merchant {risk_level: 'HIGH'})
RETURN c.name AS customer_name, c.country AS customer_country, a.account_id AS bank_account, m.name AS high_risk_merchant, m.category AS merchant_type
queries.talk_track: Here, we correlate customer profile risk with transaction endpoints. This GQL query traces the direct flow from a high-risk individual through their transactional bank accounts into high-risk merchants, such as offshore casinos. By combining profile metadata and transaction edges in one GQL execution, compliance officers can instantly flag suspicious integration patterns.

queries.title: Rapid Multi-Hop Fund Layering
queries.description: Trace rapid high-value transfers across a multi-hop chain of bank accounts to flag potential layering velocity.
queries.gql:
GRAPH AMLGraph
MATCH (a:Account)-[t1:Transfers]->(b:Account)-[t2:Transfers]->(c:Account)
WHERE t1.amount > 50000 AND t2.amount > 50000
RETURN a.account_id AS source_account, b.account_id AS intermediary_account, c.account_id AS destination_account, t1.amount AS first_transfer, t2.amount AS second_transfer
queries.talk_track: Structuring and layering involve moving large amounts of cash rapidly through multiple accounts to break the audit trail. This GQL query matches multi-hop transfers where both legs of the journey exceed high-value thresholds ($50k). Spanner Graph allows compliance teams to trace these multi-hop flows across millions of accounts globally without needing complex table joins.
